title: Quiz: Electrocatalysis
description: Test your understanding of electrochemical catalysis

questions.question: In the Computational Hydrogen Electrode (CHE), what is the reference state at 0 V vs RHE?

options.text: H⁺ + e⁻ in equilibrium with ½H₂(g)
Pure metal in its standard state

questions.explanation: The CHE uses the equilibrium H⁺ + e⁻ ↔ ½H₂(g) at 0 V vs RHE as the reference. This means G(H⁺) + G(e⁻) = ½G(H₂), providing a convenient reference for all proton-coupled electron transfer reactions.

questions.question: What is the optimal ΔG(H*) for the hydrogen evolution reaction (HER)?
questions.explanation: The optimal HER catalyst has ΔG(H*) ≈ 0 eV (thermoneutral). If binding is too strong (negative), H* cannot desorb. If too weak (positive), H* cannot form. Pt is near optimal with ΔG(H*) ≈ -0.05 eV.

questions.question: Why is there a minimum overpotential (~0.37 V) for OER catalysts?

options.text: DFT calculations are not accurate enough
The OOH-OH scaling relation constrains binding energies
Oxygen is difficult to generate electrochemically
All metals are poor OER catalysts

questions.explanation: The universal scaling relation ΔG(OOH) - ΔG(OH) ≈ 3.2 eV prevents independent optimization of these intermediates. Since ideal OER requires 4 steps of 1.23 V each, the fixed 3.2 eV gap sets a minimum theoretical overpotential.

questions.question: For CO₂ reduction, why is copper unique among metals?

options.text: It produces hydrocarbons and C₂+ products through CO coupling

questions.explanation: Copper is unique in producing hydrocarbons (CH₄) and C₂+ products (C₂H₄, C₂H₅OH) from CO₂ reduction. This is because Cu has optimal CO binding for *CO + *CO coupling before CO desorbs, enabling C-C bond formation.

questions.question: When using the RHE (Reversible Hydrogen Electrode) reference scale, pH effects on free energy are automatically included.
questions.explanation: True. The RHE scale is defined as U_RHE = U_SHE + 0.059×pH, which exactly compensates for the pH dependence of proton activity. This makes CHE calculations independent of pH when using the RHE reference.

questions.question: Which strategies can help break OER/ORR scaling relations? (Select all)

options.text: Using single-atom catalysts with unique coordination
Creating bifunctional sites for different intermediates
Using different active sites for OH* and OOH*

questions.explanation: Breaking scaling requires changing how intermediates interact with the catalyst. SACs have different coordination than bulk surfaces, bifunctional sites can stabilize intermediates differently, and different sites for different intermediates bypasses the correlation. Surface area doesn't affect scaling relations.
